column6: Please Add the Compound Indicated Here in the Window Display: "kumudvatīśaḥ the moon". This compound deserves to be a Searchable Headword, located in the Hierarchy List Display, in order to Fully Honor and Pay Resect to AP57.

column7: Typo – In Sanskrit poetics, the standard of comparison must syntactically agree in case with the subject of comparison. Princess Indumatī is the implied nominative subject who "did not fix her affection" (bhāvaṃ na babandha). Therefore, the upamāna must be the nominative singular kumudvatī, not the accusative singular kumudvatīṃ. The locative bhānumati ("in the sun") dictates the unsuitability of the object of affection, confirming the structural logic: "Like the white water-lily in the sun, she did not fix her affection [upon him]."

Typo – The true morphological reality of the word "piśaṃga" demands LB the application of Pāṇini 8.4.58 (anusvārasya yayi parasavarṇaḥ). Before the guttural stop 'g', the nasal must assimilate to the guttural class nasal 'ṅ'. Therefore, the absolute, flawless orthography is "piśaṅga" !
